title: Vitis compressa
taxonomy: Vitis compressa is classified as a species of grapevine in the family Vitaceae. It belongs to the genus Vitis, which includes other species of wild grapes. The classification of Vitis compressa is further divided into the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Angiospermophyta. This species is native to the western United States and is often found growing in woodland areas. Vitis compressa is considered a rare and endangered species in some regions due to habitat loss and other environmental factors.

intro: Vitis compressa, also known as the American grapevine, is a deciduous woody vine native to eastern North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, improve heart health, and reduce the risk of certain cancers. Bioactive constituents of Vitis compressa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and resveratrol, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Vitis compressa include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support cardiovascular health and immune function.
